assertion_results.assertion: The post-triage summary comment (Step 8) includes an @mention of the vulnerability issue's reporter using an ADF mention node with account ID '557058:psirt-analyst-mock-id' (§1.67)

assertion_results.assertion: The reporter @mention in Step 8 is present by default without any ProdSec configuration — it uses the reporter field from the Jira issue (§1.67)

assertion_results.assertion: The Affects Versions correction comment (Step 3) includes an @mention of the ProdSec contact using an ADF mention node with account ID '557058:prodsec-mock-account-id' (§1.68)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md lines 62-72: The Affects Versions correction comment includes an ADF mention node JSON block with "id": "557058:prodsec-mock-account-id" and "text": "@prodsec-team".

assertion_results.assertion: The ProdSec @mention appears before the Comment Footnote in the Affects Versions correction comment (§1.68)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md: The ADF mention node block (lines 62-72) appears before the Comment Footnote '_[sdlc-workflow:triage-security]_' (line 76). The ordering is correct.

assertion_results.assertion: The ProdSec Jira account ID from the Security Configuration is successfully extracted and used in the Affects Versions correction comment — evidenced by the @mention with account ID '557058:prodsec-mock-account-id' in Step 3 output (Step 0 config extraction → Step 3 usage)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md line 15: Step 0 config extraction shows 'ProdSec Jira account ID | 557058:prodsec-mock-account-id'. affects-versions.md line 68: Step 3 uses this value in the ADF mention node with "id": "557058:prodsec-mock-account-id". The end-to-end flow from extraction to usage is demonstrated.
